avoid.market: Will the Fed cut rates 0 times? (KXRATECUTCOUNT-26DEC31-T0)
avoid.reason: Passes volume/spread filters but no non-fabricated, dated primary source available in-scan for mid-2026 growth/inflation/labor path to justify an 8%+ macro edge versus ~79.8% implied.

avoid.market: Will the Federal Reserve cut rates before 2027? (KXRATECUT-26DEC31)
avoid.reason: Mirror of zero-cut contract; priced consistently (~20% cut). Cannot ground a contrary Fed path without fabricating forward guidance or FOMC dots not in the supplied data.

avoid.market: Will there be a recession in 2027? (KXRECSSNBER-27)
avoid.reason: Long-dated NBER-style GDP definition through 2027; 41% mid is plausible. Edge cannot be evidenced to 8% without current BEA/NFP trajectory sources beyond market-implied levels.

avoid.market: Q2/Q3 2026 GDP and July 2026 CPI ladder
avoid.reason: All individual contracts have volume under $50K filter threshold (or wide spreads on thinner CPI rungs).
Volume under $50K on nearly all strikes; residual names have insufficient liquidity for risk-adjusted entry and no spot/fix reference in supplied payload.
Same-day weather but volume well below $50K cutoff; skipped per filter rules despite tighter spreads.
Very low volume/OI and wide spreads; season still running with no in-scan best-track update to anchor edge.

notes: Only three contracts clear volume>=50k and spread<=0.10: the 2026 Fed cut pair and 2027 recession. Market-implied regime is neutral-to-mildly sticky: ~80% odds of zero 2026 cuts, sub-50% near-term deep disinflation in thin CPI tapes, mid-2% GDP, ~41% 2027 recession. Without external retrieval, no credible specific release (FOMC SEP, CPI print, BEA advance, or NWS CLI) can be cited that is not fabricated; rules require skip. Zero trades is the correct output.
